page: Amazon Web Services i7ie.metal-24xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server designed for data-intensive workloads. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 48 physical cores and 96 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The system architecture is x86_64 and includes a 320 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

A primary feature of this server is its massive local storage capacity, consisting of 60,000 GB of NVMe SSD storage. This high-density local storage is bundled directly with the bare-metal instance. For external connectivity, the server provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

The processor supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for deep learning and matrix operations. In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in Passmark database operations, Geekbench multi-core scores, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. It also achieves strong results in Redis SET operations and medium-model LLM prompt processing, though text generation for large 70B LLM models falls into the average tier due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i7ie.metal-24xl offers high resource density by combining substantial memory capacity with an exceptionally large local NVMe storage footprint on a single bare-metal node. This eliminates the need for external network-attached storage for high-throughput data operations. The server is optimized for large-scale databases, high-performance computing storage nodes, data warehousing, real-time analytics, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.metal-24xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server designed for data-intensive enterprise workloads. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 96 vCPUs (48 physical cores) running at 3.6 GHz, paired with 768.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory. The server's defining feature is its bundled 60,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, complemented by a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance in database operations, multi-core processing, and data decompression, supported by hardware-accelerated instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. This high-density hardware profile offers qualitative cost efficiency by consolidating massive storage and memory on a single physical node, reducing reliance on external storage networks. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, data warehousing, real-time analytics, and memory-intensive applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.metal-24xl is a bare-metal server featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 60,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, optimized for large-scale databases and data warehousing.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.metal-24xl is a bare-metal server with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 60,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal platform combining ninety-six vCPUs, DDR5 memory, and sixty terabytes of local NVMe storage for data-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 96 vCPUs and 3.6 GHz clock speed
768.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
60,000 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier benchmark performance in database operations and data decompression
Best suited for large-scale databases, data warehousing, and real-time analytics
